title: tool_intercept: unwrap on Option after is_none check uses brittle short-circuit pattern
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/tool_intercept.rs:316-326 (try_extract_shorthand_format)
## Violation
```rust
if let Some(idx) = self.buffer.find(&open_tag) {
    if earliest_match.is_none() || idx < earliest_match.unwrap().0 {
        earliest_match = Some((idx, *tool));
    }
}
```

The condition relies on short-circuit evaluation to avoid panic on `unwrap()`. A future refactor that swaps the order (`idx < earliest_match.unwrap().0 || earliest_match.is_none()`) introduces an unconditional panic; the compiler cannot warn.

## Mandated refactor
Replace each branch with `let should_replace = earliest_match.map_or(true, |(prev, _)| idx < prev);` or fold both scans into a single `.iter().filter_map(...).min_by_key(|(idx, _)| *idx)`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
